'''Chris Joynt''' (born 7 December 1971) is a former professional [[rugby league]] footballer who played in the 1990s and 2000s. A [[Great Britain national rugby league team|Great Britain]] international representative {{rlp|pr|sr}} and {{rlp|lf}}, he played his club football with [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]], with whom he won numerous titles, as well as [[Oldham R.L.F.C.|Oldham]] and a stint in Australia with the [[Newcastle Knights]].<ref name="Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org"/> He lifted many trophies throughout a glittering [[Knowsley Road]] career, including consecutive Super League championships in 1999 and 2000.

==Background==
Born on 7 December 1971, Joynt grew up in Beech Hill, [[Wigan]], [[Lancashire]].{{sfnp|Joynt|Critchley|2005|p=8}} He attended [[St John Fisher Catholic High School, Wigan|St John Fisher Catholic High School]].{{sfnp|Joynt|Critchley|2005|p=10}} He played junior [[rugby league]] for [[Wigan St Judes]] before joining [[Wigan St Patricks]] at the age of 14.{{sfnp|Joynt|Critchley|2005|p=12}}

After leaving school, and prior to becoming a full-time professional in rugby league, Joynt worked as a plasterer.<ref>{{cite web |title=Why I'll always be happy to pitch in |url=https://www.liverpoolecho.co.uk/sport/other-sport/ill-always-happy-pitch-3536890 |website=Liverpool Echo |access-date=25 July 2025 |date=6 December 2004}}</ref>

==Playing career==
===1990s===
Joynt signed with [[Oldham R.L.F.C.|Oldham]] in April 1989, and made his first team debut a year later, in April 1990.{{sfnp|Joynt|Critchley|2005|p=204}} In 1991, Joynt spent the English off-season in Australia, playing for NSW [[Group 20 Rugby League|Group 20]] club, Yenda Blueheelers.{{sfnp|Joynt|Critchley|2005|p=24}} After spending two years mostly in the 'A' team, he became a regular in the Oldham first team during the [[1991–92 Rugby Football League season|1991–92 season]]. He played for the club in the [[1991–92 Rugby League Divisional Premiership|1991–92 Divisional Premiership]] final at the end of the season at [[Old Trafford]], but were defeated by [[Sheffield Eagles]].

He was sold to [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]] in September 1992 in exchange for Sean Devine, Paul Jones and a fee of £50,000.<ref>{{cite web |title=Stewart earmarked for key role |url=https://www.lancashiretelegraph.co.uk/news/6045874.stewart-earmarked-key-role/ |website=Lancashire Telegraph |access-date=8 May 2023 |date=8 February 2001}}</ref><ref>{{cite news |title=Kiwi Maea injured in road crash |url=https://www.britishnewspaperarchive.co.uk/viewer/BL/0003725/19920903/043/0043 |work=Oldham Advertiser |date=3 September 1992 |page=43 |via=[[British Newspaper Archive]] |url-access=subscription}}</ref> 

Joynt played at {{rlp|sr}} in [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]] 4–5 defeat by [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]] in the [[1992–93 Lancashire Cup]] Final during the [[1992–93 Rugby Football League season|1992–93 season]] at [[Knowsley Road]], [[St Helens, Merseyside|St Helens]] on Sunday 18 October 1992.<ref name="1992-1993 Lancashire Cup Final">{{cite web|url=http://wigan.rlfans.com/news.php?readmore=2745|title=1992–1993 Lancashire Cup Final|publisher=wigan.rlfans.com|date=31 December 2014|access-date=1 January 2015|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180713170348/http://wigan.rlfans.com/news.php?readmore=2745|archive-date=13 July 2018|url-status=dead}}</ref> Joynt won his first silverware later that season in the [[1992–93 Rugby League Premiership|1992–93 Premiership]] final, with St Helens this time winning 10–4 over Wigan.<ref>{{cite web |title=Rugby League: Deserving St Helens succeed at last |url=https://www.independent.co.uk/sport/rugby-league-deserving-st-helens-succeed-at-last-wigan-fail-in-their-bid-for-a-grand-slam-but-featherstone-complete-a-double-2323403.html |website=The Independent |access-date=25 July 2025 |date=17 May 1993}}</ref>

Joynt played left-{{rlp|sr}} in [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]]' 16–25 defeat by [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]] in the [[1995–96 League Cup (rugby league)|1995–96 Regal Trophy]] Final during the [[1995–96 Rugby Football League season|1995–96]] at [[John Smith's Stadium|Alfred McAlpine Stadium]], [[Huddersfield]] on Saturday 13 January 1996.<ref name="13th January 1996: St Helens 16 Wigan 25 (Regal Trophy Final)">{{cite web|url=http://wigan.rlfans.com/news.php?readmore=2976|title=13th January 1996: St Helens 16 Wigan 25 (Regal Trophy Final)|publisher=wigan.rlfans.com|date=31 December 2014|access-date=1 January 2015|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20181007005311/http://wigan.rlfans.com/news.php?readmore=2976|archive-date=7 October 2018|url-status=dead}}</ref>

Joynt played for [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]] at {{rlp|sr}} in their [[1996 Challenge Cup]] Final victory over [[Bradford Bulls]].<ref>{{cite web|title=steveprescottfoundation.co.uk|url=http://www.steveprescottfoundation.co.uk/About/StevePrescottStats.aspx|work=Steve Prescott Stats|publisher=Steve Prescott Foundation|access-date=3 June 2012|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20120808052156/http://www.steveprescottfoundation.co.uk/About/StevePrescottStats.aspx|archive-date=8 August 2012|url-status=dead}}</ref> He took over as captain of the club in 1997, and held the position until 2003.<ref>{{cite web |title=Joynt quits captaincy |url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport1/hi/rugby_league/super_league/st_helens/3393579.stm |website=BBC Sport |access-date=25 July 2025 |date=13 January 2004}}</ref> Joynt played for [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]] at {{rlp|lf}} in their [[1999 Super League Grand Final]] victory over [[Bradford Bulls]].

===2000s===
Having won the 1999 Championship, [[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]] contested in the [[2000 World Club Challenge]] against [[National Rugby League]] Premiers the [[Melbourne Storm]], with Joynt playing at {{rlp|sr}} in the loss. Joynt is probably best remembered as the scorer of the [[Wide to West]] try in the Super League Final Eliminator in 2000, which won the match for St. Helens. He played at {{rlp|sr}} and scored two [[Try (rugby)|tries]] in the subsequent [[2000 Super League Grand Final]] victory over the [[Wigan Warriors]], and being awarded the [[Harry Sunderland Trophy]]. As [[Super League V]] champions, St. Helens played against [[2000 NRL season|2000 NRL]] Premiers, the [[Brisbane Broncos]] in the [[2001 World Club Challenge]]. Joynt was the [[Captain (sports)|captain]], played as a {{rlp|sr}} and scored a [[Try (rugby)|try]] in St. Helens' victory.

In 2002, Joynt was at the centre of controversy when he seemingly performed a '[[voluntary tackle]]' with seconds remaining in the [[2002 Super League Grand Final]]. St. Helens had just kicked a [[drop-goal]] to take a one-point lead in the match, and the penalty from the voluntary tackle would have given the [[Bradford Bulls]] a chance to kick at goal and win the match. However, the referee dismissed wild protests from the Bradford Bulls players and the final hooter went, with St. Helens winning 19–18.<ref>{{cite web |last1=Wilson |first1=Andy |title=Lowes rages as Saints clinch title thriller |url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2002/oct/21/rugbyleague.sthelens |website=The Guardian |access-date=25 July 2025 |date=21 October 2002}}</ref> Having won [[Super League VI]], St. Helens contested the [[2003 World Club Challenge]] against 2002 NRL Premiers, the [[Sydney Roosters]]. Joynt captained St. Helens as a {{rlp|sr}} in their 0-38 defeat. Joynt relinquished captaincy of St. Helens to [[Paul Sculthorpe]] at the end of 2003's [[Super League VIII]], and retired from rugby league following the 2004's [[Super League IX]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.independent.co.uk/sport/general/rugby-league/joynt-calls-time-on-career-at-st-helens-535068.html|title=Joynt calls time on career at St Helens|work=The Independent|date=29 November 2004|access-date=3 June 2016}}</ref>

==International career==
Joynt represented [[England national rugby league team|England]] at the [[1995 Rugby League World Cup]]. He was selected to play for [[England national rugby league team|England]] in the [[1995 Rugby League World Cup|1995 World Cup]] Final on the reserve bench but [[Australia national rugby league team|Australia]] won the match and retained the Cup. 

Joynt was a [[Great Britain national rugby league team|Great Britain]] international with 25 caps. In the 1997 post season, Joynt was selected to play for [[Great Britain national rugby league team|Great Britain]] at {{rlp|sr}} in all three matches of the [[Super League Test series]] against [[Australia national rugby league team|Australia]].

Joynt was eligible to play for [[Ireland national rugby league team|Ireland]] through his Irish grandmother,{{sfnp|Joynt|Critchley|2005|p=131}} and represented the team at the [[2000 Rugby League World Cup]]. 

Joynt also represented [[Lancashire rugby league team|Lancashire]] in the [[Origin Series]].
